title: calycobolus sericeus
intro: Calycobolus sericeus is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Convolvulaceae family and commonly known as the silky bindweed or silky calycobolus. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly due to its antimalarial, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Calycobolus sericeus include its ability to treat fever, malaria, and other infectious diseases, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Calycobolus sericeus can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
intro_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", calycobolus sericeus has immunostimulatory benefits, as it was found to possess higher immunostimulatory activities compared to levamisole, a positive control, and exhibited significant dose-dependent immunostimulatory activity.
